---
title: "Demographic Summary"
jupyter: python3
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Demographic Characteristics

### Population

In 2023, New York State’s (NYS) population was nearly 20 million. When excluding New York City (NYC), the population was 11,356,117. The Mid-Hudson Region (M-H Region) made up 12.1% of NYS’ population and includes the seven counties of Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Sullivan, Ulster, and Westchester. Westchester County comprised the largest portion of the M-H Region’s population at 41.6%, while Sullivan County made up only 3.3% of the M-H Region [see @tbl-population-demographics].

The population of the M-H Region grew 4.7% from 2010 to 2020. In those 10 years, growth increased most rapidly in Rockland (7.9%) and Orange (7.1%). Putnam (-2.1%), and Ulster (-0.4%) had negative growth.<a href="#cite-7" title="United States Census Bureau, 2025, https://data.census.gov/cedsci/table?q=decennial%20population&g=0500000US36027%248600000,36071%248600000,36079%248600000,36087%248600000,36105%248600000,36111%248600000,36119%248600000&d=DEC%20Summary%20File%201&tid=DECENNIALSF12010.P1, accessed August 2025"><sup>7</sup></a>

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-population-demographics
#| tbl-cap: "Population Demographic Characteristics, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data
data = {
    " ": [
        "Dutchess",
        "Orange", 
        "Putnam",
        "Rockland",
        "Sullivan",
        "Ulster",
        "Westchester",
        "Mid-Hudson (Total)",
        "NYS excl NYC",
        "NYS (Total)"
    ],
    "Total Population": [
        297144,
        403840,
        97988,
        338936,
        79147,
        182109,
        996888,
        2396052,
        11356117,
        19872319
    ],
    "Percent of Mid-Hudson Region": [
        12.4,
        16.9,
        4.1,
        14.1,
        3.3,
        7.6,
        41.6,
        100.0,
        np.nan,
        np.nan
    ],
    "Percent of NYS": [
        1.5,
        2.0,
        0.5,
        1.7,
        0.4,
        0.9,
        5.0,
        12.1,
        57.1,
        100.0
    ]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
df["Total Population"] = df["Total Population"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
df["Percent of Mid-Hudson Region"] = df["Percent of Mid-Hudson Region"].apply(
    lambda x: f"{x:.1f}" if pd.notna(x) else "N/A"
)
df["Percent of NYS"] = df["Percent of NYS"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling
styled_table = style_cha_table(df)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, [Table S0101](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S0101?q=s0101&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Sex

When stratifying the population by sex in 2023, the M-H Region had a near-even distribution between males and females [see @tbl-sex]. Apart from Putnam and Sullivan Counties, the M-H Region had a slightly higher percentage of females than males. The same is true for NYS, as well as NYS excluding NYC.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-sex
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Sex, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): [
        "Dutchess",
        "Orange",
        "Putnam",
        "Rockland",
        "Sullivan",
        "Ulster",
        "Westchester",
        "Mid-Hudson",
        "NYS excl NYC",
        "NYS"
    ],
    ("Male", "Total Population"): [
        147902,
        201512,
        49503,
        167624,
        40993,
        90719,
        485985,
        1184238,
        5614391,
        9702417
    ],
    ("Male", "%"): [
        49.8,
        49.9,
        50.5,
        49.5,
        51.8,
        49.8,
        48.8,
        49.4,
        49.4,
        48.8
    ],
    ("Female", "Total Population"): [
        149242,
        202328,
        48485,
        171312,
        38154,
        91390,
        510903,
        1211814,
        5741726,
        10169902
    ],
    ("Female", "%"): [
        50.2,
        50.1,
        49.5,
        50.5,
        48.2,
        50.2,
        51.2,
        50.6,
        50.6,
        51.2
    ]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The American Community Survey includes a question that intends to capture current sex; there are no questions about gender, sexual orientation, or sex at birth. Respondents should respond either "male" or "female" based on how they currently identify their sex.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, [Table S0101](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S0101?q=s0101&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Age

Throughout the M-H Region, adults aged 50 to 59 years made up the largest portion of the population (13.8%) [see @tbl-age]. Children aged less than five years and five to nine years, as well as adults aged 40 to 49 years and 60 to 69 years, were similarly distributed throughout the M-H Region, with Ulster County having the greatest difference between adults aged 60 to 69 years and children less than five years old (14.4% vs 4.4%).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-age
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Age, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("<5 years", "Total Population"): [13613, 27143, 4521, 28957, 4656, 7926, 53282, 140098, 604728, 1102961],
    ("<5 years", "%"): [4.6, 6.7, 4.6, 8.5, 5.9, 4.4, 5.3, 5.8, 5.3, 5.6],
    ("5-9 years", "Total Population"): [14448, 27679, 4320, 27051, 4969, 8802, 57324, 144593, 640751, 1102946],
    ("5-9 years", "%"): [4.9, 6.9, 4.4, 8.0, 6.3, 4.8, 5.8, 6.0, 5.6, 5.6],
    ("10-19 years", "Total Population"): [37031, 61718, 12426, 52692, 9343, 19831, 129780, 322821, 1454784, 2413200],
    ("10-19 years", "%"): [12.5, 15.3, 12.7, 15.5, 11.8, 10.9, 13.0, 13.5, 12.8, 12.1],
    ("20-29 years", "Total Population"): [38178, 52658, 10922, 41742, 9032, 22175, 115269, 289976, 1442397, 2658703],
    ("20-29 years", "%"): [12.8, 13.0, 11.1, 12.3, 11.4, 12.2, 11.6, 12.1, 12.7, 13.4],
    ("30-39 years", "Total Population"): [35520, 48459, 11264, 38885, 9668, 23060, 121312, 288168, 1393651, 2751639],
    ("30-39 years", "%"): [12.0, 12.0, 11.5, 11.5, 12.2, 12.7, 12.2, 12.0, 12.3, 13.8],
    ("40-49 years", "Total Population"): [36796, 49782, 13032, 36560, 9486, 22177, 133617, 301450, 1343897, 2422399],
    ("40-49 years", "%"): [12.4, 12.3, 13.3, 10.8, 12.0, 12.2, 13.4, 12.6, 11.8, 12.2],
    ("50-59 years", "Total Population"): [43025, 52995, 15538, 40115, 11009, 26342, 141210, 330234, 1566110, 2638363],
    ("50-59 years", "%"): [14.5, 13.1, 15.9, 11.8, 13.9, 14.5, 14.2, 13.8, 13.8, 13.3],
    ("60-69 years", "Total Population"): [41707, 45285, 13386, 35529, 10980, 26250, 121875, 295012, 1485429, 2425309],
    ("60-69 years", "%"): [14.0, 11.2, 13.7, 10.5, 13.9, 14.4, 12.2, 12.3, 13.1, 12.2],
    ("70-79 years", "Total Population"): [23935, 25081, 8739, 23713, 7087, 16546, 74727, 179828, 919800, 1511287],
    ("70-79 years", "%"): [8.1, 6.2, 8.9, 7.0, 9.0, 9.1, 7.5, 7.5, 8.1, 7.6],
    (">80 years", "Total Population"): [12891, 13040, 3840, 13692, 2917, 9000, 48492, 103872, 504570, 845512],
    (">80 years", "%"): [4.3, 3.2, 3.9, 4.0, 3.7, 4.9, 4.9, 4.3, 4.4, 4.3],
    ("<18 years", "Total Population"): [55342, 103740, 19180, 99733, 16947, 31636, 214739, 541317, 2369080, 4109277],
    ("<18 years", "%"): [18.6, 25.7, 19.6, 29.4, 21.4, 17.4, 21.5, 22.6, 20.9, 20.7]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, [Table S0101](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S0101?q=s0101&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Ethnicity and Race

In 2023, the majority of the population in the M-H Region and NYS were non-Hispanic White (58.5% and 53.4%, respectively). The Hispanic population was the second most predominant racial/ethnic group, followed by the non-Hispanic Black population. Within the M-H Region, Westchester County had the highest Hispanic population (27.0%), the highest non-Hispanic Black population (12.9%), and the highest non-Hispanic Asian population (6.0%). Westchester County’s racial/ethnic profile is most like that of NYS’; however, the percentage of non-Hispanic Whites significantly increases when looking at NYS excluding NYC [see @tbl-ethnicity and @tbl-race].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-ethnicity
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Ethnicity, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("Hispanic", "Total Population"): [44153, 93608, 18676, 67951, 14544, 21562, 269085, 529579, 1478113, 3898652],
    ("Hispanic", "%"): [14.9, 23.2, 19.1, 20.0, 18.4, 11.8, 27.0, 22.1, 13.0, 19.6],
    ("Non-Hispanic", "Total Population"): [252991, 310232, 79312, 270985, 64603, 160547, 727803, 1866473, 9878004, 15973667],
    ("Non-Hispanic", "%"): [85.1, 76.8, 80.9, 80.0, 81.6, 88.2, 73.0, 77.9, 87.0, 80.4]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{int(x):,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The Census Bureau collects ethnic data in accordance with guidelines provided by the US Office of Management and Budget (OMB) and these data are based on self-identification. For ethnicity, the OMB standards classify individuals in one of two categories: "Hispanic or Latino" or "Not Hispanic or Latino." The Census Bureau uses the term "Hispanic or Latino" interchangeably with the term "Hispanic," and also refer to this concept as "ethnicity.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, [Table B03002](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSDT5Y2023.B03002?q=b03002&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-race
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Race, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("Non-Hispanic White", "Total Population"): [199670, 239186, 70948, 205539, 53145, 133874, 498855, 1401217, 7942876, 10608842],
    ("Non-Hispanic White", "%"): [67.2, 59.2, 72.4, 60.6, 67.1, 73.5, 50.0, 58.5, 69.9, 53.4],
    ("Non-Hispanic Black or African American", "Total Population"): [28024, 42478, 2894, 35626, 6263, 10347, 128199, 253831, 934322, 2708094],
    ("Non-Hispanic Black or African American", "%"): [9.4, 10.5, 3.0, 10.5, 7.9, 5.7, 12.9, 10.6, 8.2, 13.6],
    ("Non-Hispanic American Indian and Alaskan Native", "Total Population"): [222, 438, 81, 181, 120, 66, 1197, 2305, 21069, 37212],
    ("Non-Hispanic American Indian and Alaskan Native", "%"): [0.07, 0.11, 0.08, 0.05, 0.15, 0.04, 0.12, 0.10, 0.19, 0.19],
    ("Non-Hispanic Asian", "Total Population"): [10044, 11223, 2255, 19772, 1584, 3675, 59912, 108465, 518716, 1754957],
    ("Non-Hispanic Asian", "%"): [3.4, 2.8, 2.3, 5.8, 2.0, 2.0, 6.0, 4.5, 4.6, 8.8],
    ("Non-Hispanic Native Hawaiian and Other Pacific Islander", "Total Population"): [117, 26, 24, 38, 11, 40, 96, 352, 2610, 6220],
    ("Non-Hispanic Native Hawaiian and Other Pacific Islander", "%"): [0.04, 0.01, 0.02, 0.01, 0.01, 0.02, 0.01, 0.01, 0.02, 0.03],
    ("Non-Hispanic Other", "Total Population"): [2206, 2563, 788, 1921, 533, 2242, 10638, 20891, 70553, 178956],
    ("Non-Hispanic Other", "%"): [0.7, 0.6, 0.8, 0.6, 0.7, 1.2, 1.1, 0.9, 0.6, 0.9],
    ("Non-Hispanic Two or More Races", "Total Population"): [12708, 14318, 2322, 7908, 2947, 10303, 28906, 79412, 387858, 679386],
    ("Non-Hispanic Two or More Races", "%"): [4.3, 3.5, 2.4, 2.3, 3.7, 5.7, 2.9, 3.3, 3.4, 3.4]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            # Handle small percentages with appropriate decimal places
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}" if x < 1.0 else f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates, [Table B03002](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSDT5Y2023.B03002?q=b03002&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Spoken Language

According to the *American Community Survey*, the base population for the spoken language demographic category was people aged five years and older. Of this population, English was the most common spoken language in the M-H Region and NYS. A significant portion of the population spoke a language other than English at home, specifically in Rockland and Westchester Counties (43.6% and 34.3%, respectively). The Spanish speaking population was highest in Westchester County (21.0%) compared to the other counties in the M-H Region [see @tbl-language].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-language
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Spoken Language, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("Only English", "Total Population"): [238585, 266545, 73860, 174907, 59484, 152966, 619503, 1585850, 8808643, 13017480],
    ("Only English", "%"): [84.1, 70.8, 79.0, 56.4, 79.9, 87.8, 65.7, 54.2, 69.4, 69.4],
    ("Language other than English", "Total Population"): [44946, 110152, 19607, 135072, 15007, 21217, 324103, 670104, 1942746, 5751878],
    ("Language other than English", "%"): [15.9, 29.2, 21.0, 43.6, 20.1, 12.2, 34.3, 22.9, 15.3, 30.6],
    ("Spanish", "Total Population"): [26003, 55418, 11733, 46360, 8639, 11924, 196271, 356348, 940527, 2762664],
    ("Spanish", "%"): [9.2, 15.0, 13.0, 15.0, 12.0, 6.8, 21.0, 12.2, 12.2, 15.0],
    ("Other Indo-European languages", "Total Population"): [10769, 45346, 5687, 69600, 4877, 5602, 78915, 220796, 632717, 1679571],
    ("Other Indo-European languages", "%"): [3.8, 12.0, 6.1, 22.5, 6.5, 3.2, 8.4, 7.5, 5.0, 8.9],
    ("Asian and Pacific Islander languages", "Total Population"): [5267, 6298, 1504, 12024, 819, 2679, 32889, 61480, 258104, 958133],
    ("Asian and Pacific Islander languages", "%"): [1.9, 1.7, 1.6, 3.9, 1.1, 1.5, 3.5, 2.1, 2.0, 5.1],
    ("Other languages", "Total Population"): [2907, 3090, 683, 7088, 672, 1012, 16028, 31480, 111398, 351510],
    ("Other languages", "%"): [1.0, 0.8, 0.7, 2.3, 0.9, 0.6, 1.7, 1.1, 0.9, 1.9]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The American Community Survey asks respondents to report whether they sometimes or always spoke a language other than English at home. People who spoke languages other than English but did not use them at home, who only used them elsewhere, or whose usage was limited to a few expressions or slang were excluded.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1601](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S1601?q=s1601&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Educational Attainment

According to the *American Community Survey*, the base population for the educational attainment demographic category were people aged 25 years and older [see @tbl-education-base]. Of this population, when looking at the M-H Region, NYS, and NYS excluding NYC, the largest portion of residents had a high school degree (22.4%, 24.6%, and 25.8%, respectively) [see @tbl-education-base].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-education-base
#| tbl-cap: "Population 25 Years and Older, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data
data = {
    " ": ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    "Total Population": [211380, 258632, 71149, 208594, 55761, 134325, 697202, 1637043, 7905485, 13996138]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
df["Total Population"] = df["Total Population"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")

# Apply standard CHA styling
styled_table = style_cha_table(df)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The Census Bureau defines educational attainment as the highest level of education that an individual has completed. This is distinct from the level of schooling that an individual is attending.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1501](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2020.S1501?q=s1501&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36&tid=ACSST5Y2020.S1501), April 2025
:::

Within the seven counties of the M-H Region, Westchester, Putnam, and Rockland Counties had the highest percentage of people with bachelor’s degrees (26.0%, 24.2%, and 23.2%, respectively), while Sullivan had the lowest percentage (17.0 %). Sullivan, Orange, and Dutchess Counties had the highest percentage of people with associate degrees (10.3%, 10.3%, and 10.1%, respectively). A significant portion of the population in the M-H Region were high school graduates or held a bachelor's degree [see @tbl-education].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-education
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Educational Attainment, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("Less than 9th grade", "N"): [6262, 10091, 2255, 11800, 2755, 3463, 37761, 74387, 300738, 836124],
    ("Less than 9th grade", "%"): [3.0, 3.9, 3.2, 5.7, 4.9, 2.6, 5.4, 4.5, 3.8, 6.0],
    ("9th to 12th grade, no diploma", "N"): [11055, 16609, 2536, 13858, 4129, 7622, 36459, 92268, 403754, 862413],
    ("9th to 12th grade, no diploma", "%"): [5.2, 6.4, 3.6, 6.6, 7.4, 5.7, 5.2, 5.6, 5.1, 6.2],
    ("High school graduate or equivalent", "N"): [52992, 72466, 17647, 45118, 16623, 37570, 124954, 367370, 2039454, 3437438],
    ("High school graduate or equivalent", "%"): [25.1, 28.0, 24.8, 21.6, 29.8, 28.0, 17.9, 22.4, 25.8, 24.6],
    ("Some college, no degree", "N"): [35689, 49355, 11728, 33790, 9973, 22968, 87534, 251037, 1277348, 2081783],
    ("Some college, no degree", "%"): [16.9, 19.1, 16.5, 16.2, 17.9, 17.1, 12.6, 15.3, 16.2, 14.9],
    ("Associate degree", "N"): [21410, 26722, 6002, 16169, 5728, 12934, 44393, 133358, 847708, 1242877],
    ("Associate degree", "%"): [10.1, 10.3, 8.4, 7.8, 10.3, 9.6, 6.4, 8.1, 10.7, 8.9],
    ("Bachelor's degree", "N"): [44414, 46977, 17222, 48405, 9476, 27094, 181482, 375070, 1643943, 3083769],
    ("Bachelor's degree", "%"): [21.0, 18.2, 24.2, 23.2, 17.0, 20.2, 26.0, 22.9, 20.8, 22.0],
    ("Graduate or professional degree", "N"): [39558, 36412, 13759, 39454, 7077, 22674, 184619, 343553, 1392540, 2451734],
    ("Graduate or professional degree", "%"): [18.7, 14.1, 19.3, 18.9, 12.7, 16.9, 26.5, 21.0, 17.6, 17.5]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "N":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The Census Bureau defines educational attainment as the highest level of education that an individual has completed. This is distinct from the level of schooling that an individual is attending.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1501](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2020.S1501?q=s1501&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36&tid=ACSST5Y2020.S1501), April 2025
:::

### Income

Income can affect many aspects of life. This includes where people are able to live, the food and health care coverage available, and almost every other social determinant of health.<a href="#cite-8" title="Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, 2013, https://www.rwjf.org/en/library/research/2012/12/how-does-employment--or-unemployment--affect-health-.html, accessed August 2025"><sup>8</sup></a>

According to the *American Community Survey*, the base population for the income demographic category were households (all the persons who occupy a housing unit as their usual place of residence) [see @tbl-households].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-households
#| tbl-cap: "Total Households, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data
data = {
    " ": ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    "Households": [115184, 137311, 35054, 103284, 30215, 73105, 370256, 864409, 4355640, 7668956]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
df["Households"] = df["Households"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")

# Apply standard CHA styling
styled_table = style_cha_table(df)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The American Community Survey defines a household as all the people who occupy a housing unit, such as a house or apartment, as their usual place of residence (the place where they live most of the time). This includes both related family members and any unrelated people living in the same unit.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1901](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S1901?q=s1901&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

Of this population, the largest portion of households in the M-H Region had an income greater than \$100,000 in 2023. More than one fifth of the households in Putnam County were making between \$100,000 and \$149,999 in 2023 (20.9%). There were many households with an income between \$50,000 and \$74,999 in the M-H Region and NYS; 17.0% of households in Sullivan County had an income within this bracket [see @tbl-income].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-income
#| tbl-cap: "Households by Income, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("<$10,000", "Total Population"): [4723, 5492, 841, 3822, 1420, 4021, 17402, 37721, 201885, 437130],
    ("<$10,000", "%"): [4.1, 4.0, 2.4, 3.7, 4.7, 5.5, 4.7, 4.4, 4.6, 5.7],
    ("$10,000-$14,999", "Total Population"): [2649, 4119, 631, 2169, 1481, 2778, 9997, 23824, 142135, 314427],
    ("$10,000-$14,999", "%"): [2.3, 3.0, 1.8, 2.1, 4.9, 3.8, 2.7, 2.8, 3.3, 4.1],
    ("$15,000-$24,999", "Total Population"): [5298, 8651, 1472, 5784, 2327, 4606, 17032, 45169, 266550, 498482],
    ("$15,000-$24,999", "%"): [4.6, 6.3, 4.2, 5.6, 7.7, 6.3, 4.6, 5.2, 6.1, 6.5],
    ("$25,000-$34,999", "Total Population"): [6105, 7003, 1402, 5474, 2447, 4752, 15180, 42364, 266736, 475475],
    ("$25,000-$34,999", "%"): [5.3, 5.1, 4.0, 5.3, 8.1, 6.5, 4.1, 4.9, 6.1, 6.2],
    ("$35,000-$49,999", "Total Population"): [9906, 10985, 2033, 7746, 3384, 7384, 27029, 68467, 402990, 697875],
    ("$35,000-$49,999", "%"): [8.6, 8.0, 5.8, 7.5, 11.2, 10.1, 7.3, 7.9, 9.3, 9.1],
    ("$50,000-$74,999", "Total Population"): [15780, 18125, 3926, 11878, 5137, 10527, 37766, 103139, 617645, 1058316],
    ("$50,000-$74,999", "%"): [13.7, 13.2, 11.2, 11.5, 17.0, 14.4, 10.2, 11.9, 14.2, 13.8],
    ("$75,000-$99,999", "Total Population"): [14513, 16340, 3365, 10328, 3807, 8334, 36285, 92973, 516423, 874261],
    ("$75,000-$99,999", "%"): [12.6, 11.9, 9.6, 10.0, 12.6, 11.4, 9.8, 10.8, 11.9, 11.4],
    ("$100,000-$149,999", "Total Population"): [22346, 27188, 7326, 18281, 4442, 13378, 59241, 152202, 781447, 1288385],
    ("$100,000-$149,999", "%"): [19.4, 19.8, 20.9, 17.7, 14.7, 18.3, 16.0, 17.6, 17.9, 16.8],
    ("$150,000-$199,999", "Total Population"): [13822, 18125, 5503, 12394, 2478, 7384, 39988, 99694, 466426, 774565],
    ("$150,000-$199,999", "%"): [12.0, 13.2, 15.7, 12.0, 8.2, 10.1, 10.8, 11.5, 10.7, 10.1],
    (">$200,000", "Total Population"): [20042, 21421, 8553, 25408, 3293, 9942, 110336, 198996, 701072, 1257709],
    (">$200,000", "%"): [17.4, 15.6, 24.4, 24.6, 10.9, 13.6, 29.8, 23.0, 16.1, 16.4]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```
::: {.callout-note}

## Note

The American Community Survey asks respondents their income in the past 12 months. Data are provided as a percentage of total households in [Table S1901](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S1901?q=s1901&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36). Calculations were made to provide data as a number in Table 9.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1901](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S1901?q=s1901&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Veteran Status

Veteran status includes men and women who served, but are not currently serving, on active duty in the United States (US) Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, or the Coast Guard, or who served in the US Merchant Marines during World War II. Some issues that veterans experience following their service include finding a new career path, reestablishing themselves in society and families, and seeking treatment for mental health issues.<a href="#cite-9" title="Blinded Veterans Association, 2019, https://bva.org/challenges-veterans-face-when-leaving-the-military/#:~:text=Many%20veterans%20struggle%20to%20find%20work%20after%20they,the%20education%20that%20is%20necessary%20for%20many%20jobs, accessed August 2025"><sup>9</sup></a> In the M-H Region, Sullivan County had the highest percentage of civilian veterans (6.0%), double the percentage of civilian veterans in Rockland and Westchester Counties (3.0% in each) [see @tbl-veteran]. Overall, there is a smaller percentage of civilian veterans in the M-H Region compared to NYS excluding NYC (3.9% vs 5.3%, respectively).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-veteran
#| tbl-cap: "Population by Veteran Status, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data with MultiIndex columns for merged headers
data = {
    (" ", ""): ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS excl NYC", "NYS"],
    ("Civilian Population 18 Years and Older", "Total Population"): [241517, 296319, 78712, 239088, 62181, 150409, 781837, 1850063, 8964773, 15737168],
    ("Civilian Veterans", "Total Population"): [11694, 16695, 3320, 7130, 3713, 6711, 23744, 73007, 478417, 607728],
    ("Civilian Veterans", "%"): [4.8, 5.6, 4.2, 3.0, 6.0, 4.5, 3.0, 3.9, 5.3, 3.9]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data
for col in df.columns:
    if col[0] != " ":  # Skip the first column
        if col[1] == "Total Population":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
        elif col[1] == "%":
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")
        elif col[1] == "":  # Single column like "Civilian Population 18 Years and Older"
            df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")

# Apply standard CHA styling with multilevel headers
styled_table = style_cha_table(df, has_multilevel_headers=True)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

The American Community Survey asks respondents if they have ever served on active duty in the US Armed Forces.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2023 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S2101](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSST5Y2023.S2101?q=s2101+&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

### Disability

According to the World Health Organization (WHO), disability bears three dimensions: impairment to body structure or mental function; activity limitation, such as difficulty hearing, moving, or problem-solving; and participation restrictions in daily activities, such as working, engaging in social or recreational activities, or accessing health care and preventive services.<a href="#cite-10" title="Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Disability and Health Overview, 2025, https://www.cdc.gov/disability-and-health/about/?CDC_AAref_Val=https://www.cdc.gov/ncbddd/disabilityandhealth/disability.html, accessed August 2025"><sup>10</sup></a> Adults with a disability typically have higher rates of chronic disease, such as obesity, heart disease, and diabetes.<a href="#cite-11" title="New York State Department of Health, 2019, https://www.health.ny.gov/statistics/prevention/injury_prevention/information_for_action/docs/2019-12_ifa_report.pdf, accessed August 2025"><sup>11</sup></a> Structural and societal barriers can limit the ability to participate in work, recreation, and programs aimed at promoting healthy living for those living with a disability.

Various types of disabilities can affect an individual’s quality of life. Types of disability include:

-   Independent living disability – difficulty performing tasks or errands alone, such as visiting a doctor’s office or shopping due to a physical, mental, or emotional condition

-   Cognitive disability – serious difficulty concentrating, remembering, or making decisions due to a physical, mental, or emotional condition

-   Self-care disability – difficulty handling tasks, such as dressing or bathing on one’s own

-   Ambulatory disability – difficulty moving around physically, such as walking or climbing stairs

-   Hearing disability – deafness or serious difficulty hearing

-   Vision disability – blindness or serious difficulty seeing, even when wearing glasses

In the M-H Region, Sullivan County had the highest percentage of adults living with a disability (15.9%), as well as the highest percentage of adults living with each of the six types of disabilities; Rockland County had the lowest percentage of adults living with a disability (8.7%) [see @tbl-disability].

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| label: tbl-disability
#| tbl-cap: "Percentage of Population by Type of Disability, 2023"
import sys
import os
# Add project root to path
cwd = os.getcwd()
# Try current directory first
if os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    if cwd not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, cwd)
# Try parent directory (if running from chapters/)
elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(cwd, '..', 'scripts', 'cha_table_styling.py')):
    parent = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(cwd, '..'))
    if parent not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, parent)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scripts.cha_table_styling import style_cha_table

# Create the data
data = {
    " ": ["Dutchess", "Orange", "Putnam", "Rockland", "Sullivan", "Ulster", "Westchester", "Mid-Hudson", "NYS"],
    "Total with Any Disability": [12.2, 11.7, 9.6, 8.7, 15.9, 14.4, 9.5, 10.2, 11.6],
    "Hearing Difficulty": [3.5, 3.2, 2.8, 2.7, 4.4, 4.2, 2.5, 2.8, 2.8],
    "Vision Difficulty": [2.0, 2.1, 1.4, 1.4, 3.0, 2.3, 1.6, 1.7, 2.1],
    "Cognitive Difficulty": [4.7, 5.4, 3.5, 3.4, 6.4, 5.1, 3.7, 3.8, 4.5],
    "Ambulatory Difficulty": [6.3, 6.3, 5.4, 4.4, 9.1, 7.4, 5.3, 5.2, 6.6],
    "Self-care Difficulty": [2.6, 2.9, 2.4, 2.2, 3.7, 3.0, 2.3, 2.3, 2.7],
    "Independent Living Difficulty": [5.9, 6.2, 4.6, 4.5, 7.2, 6.0, 4.8, 3.9, 5.7]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Format the data - all are percentages
for col in df.columns:
    if col != " ":
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1f}")

# Apply standard CHA styling
styled_table = style_cha_table(df)
styled_table
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

Respondents who report any one of the six disability types are considered to have a disability in the American Community Survey.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Source

US Census Bureau; American Community Survey, 2020 American Community Survey 5-year estimates, [Table S1810](https://data.census.gov/table?q=s1810&g=050XX00US36105,36027,36071,36119,36087,36079,36111_160XX00US3651000_040XX00US36), April 2025
:::

## References

<details>
<summary><strong>Click to view citations</strong></summary>

<span id="cite-7">7. United States Census Bureau, 2025, <https://data.census.gov/cedsci/table?q=decennial%20population&g=0500000US36027%248600000,36071%248600000,36079%248600000,36087%248600000,36105%248600000,36111%248600000,36119%248600000&d=DEC%20Summary%20File%201&tid=DECENNIALSF12010.P1>, accessed August 2025</span>

<span id="cite-8">8. Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, 2013, <https://www.rwjf.org/en/library/research/2012/12/how-does-employment--or-unemployment--affect-health-.html>, accessed August 2025</span>

<span id="cite-9">9. Blinded Veterans Association, 2019, <https://bva.org/challenges-veterans-face-when-leaving-the-military/#:~:text=Many%20veterans%20struggle%20to%20find%20work%20after%20they,the%20education%20that%20is%20necessary%20for%20many%20jobs>, accessed August 2025</span>

<span id="cite-10">10. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Disability and Health Overview, 2025, <https://www.cdc.gov/disability-and-health/about/?CDC_AAref_Val=https://www.cdc.gov/ncbddd/disabilityandhealth/disability.html>, accessed August 2025</span>

<span id="cite-11">11. New York State Department of Health, 2019, <https://www.health.ny.gov/statistics/prevention/injury_prevention/information_for_action/docs/2019-12_ifa_report.pdf>, accessed August 2025</span>

</details>
